name: Wallet SDK Selection
description: Choose the right wallet integration for your Solana project

tree.question: What type of application are you building?

Web dApp (Next.js, React).question: Do you need social login (email, Google, Apple, SMS)?

Yes — consumer app, onboard non-crypto u.rationale: Privy provides embedded wallets + social login. Users don't need a browser extension. Best for consumer apps targeting non-crypto-native users.
Yes — consumer app, onboard non-crypto u.setup: Wrap app in <PrivyProvider appId='YOUR_APP_ID'>. Get appId from dashboard.privy.io.
Yes — consumer app, onboard non-crypto u.tradeoffs: Privy is a paid service. Free tier: 1000 MAU. Vendor lock-in on wallet custody.

No — users already have Solana wallets.question: Do you need to support multiple wallet providers (Phantom, Solflare, Backpack, etc.)?

Yes — support all wallets.rationale: The standard Solana wallet adapter supports 10+ wallet providers. Most Solana dApps use this.
Yes — support all wallets.setup: Wrap app in <ConnectionProvider> + <WalletProvider> + <WalletModalProvider>. Add <WalletMultiButton /> for connect UI.
Yes — support all wallets.tradeoffs: Users must have a browser extension wallet installed. More wallets = more edge cases to test.

No — Phantom only is fine.rationale: Direct Phantom integration via the Phantom Browser SDK. Simpler setup, fewer dependencies. 80%+ of Solana users have Phantom.
No — Phantom only is fine.setup: Use createPhantom() from @phantom/browser-sdk for embedded wallet or deeplink connection. For wallet-adapter projects, use new PhantomWalletAdapter() from @solana/wallet-adapter-wallets.
No — Phantom only is fine.tradeoffs: Only works with Phantom. If Phantom goes down or user prefers Solflare, they can't use your app.

AI Agent / Bot / Server.question: Does the agent need a persistent wallet that survives restarts?

Yes — long-running agent with its own wa.rationale: Privy server-side embedded wallets persist across restarts. No key management headaches. Agent Kit has native Privy support.
Yes — long-running agent with its own wa.setup: Create server wallet via Privy API. Pass wallet to SolanaAgentKit constructor.
Yes — long-running agent with its own wa.tradeoffs: Privy custody means you don't hold keys directly. Monthly cost at scale.

No — ephemeral or file-based keypair is.rationale: Standard Solana keypair file. Full control, zero vendor dependency. Use for bots, scripts, CLI tools.
No — ephemeral or file-based keypair is.setup: solana-keygen new --outfile agent-wallet.json. Load with Keypair.fromSecretKey().
No — ephemeral or file-based keypair is.tradeoffs: You manage key security. If the file leaks, funds are gone. No social recovery.

Mobile App (React Native).rationale: Solana Mobile Wallet Adapter is the standard for React Native. Connects to any installed mobile wallet (Phantom, Solflare).
Mobile App (React Native).setup: Use transact() from mobile-wallet-adapter-protocol to connect and sign.
Mobile App (React Native).tradeoffs: Android-first. iOS support exists but less mature. Users need a mobile wallet app installed.

Native Mobile (Kotlin/Swift).rationale: Use solana-kotlin-compose for Android or Solana Swift SDK for iOS.
Native Mobile (Kotlin/Swift).install: Android: add solana-kotlin-compose dependency. iOS: add SolanaSwift package.
Native Mobile (Kotlin/Swift).setup: Follow platform-specific scaffold. Both connect to Phantom or Solflare mobile.
Native Mobile (Kotlin/Swift).tradeoffs: Smaller ecosystem than React Native. Fewer tutorials and community support.

CLI tool / Script.rationale: Direct keypair for CLI tools. No UI needed.
CLI tool / Script.install: npm install @solana/kit (or use solana CLI directly)
CLI tool / Script.tradeoffs: Key file must be protected. Consider using solana config set --keypair for convenience.
